selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "Creating a robust AI-powered content moderation system is crucial for addressing the issue of fake content on social media. This system should be able to identify and flag false information in real-time, while also providing users with ways to report and remove such content. Additionally, platforms must invest in developing more sophisticated AI algorithms that can detect and prevent the spread of misinformation, rather than relying solely on human moderators who may not always be able to keep up with the sheer volume of content being shared online. Furthermore, social media companies must also work towards creating a culture of responsibility and accountability among their users, by promoting critical thinking and media literacy skills, and encouraging users to fact-check information before sharing it.",
"reasoning_summary": "To create an effective AI-powered content moderation system, we must first identify the key factors that contribute to the spread of misinformation on social media. This includes understanding how AI algorithms can be used to detect and flag false information, as well as developing more sophisticated AI models that can predict the likelihood of a piece of content being fake. Once these factors are understood, we can begin to develop and implement a robust content moderation system that is capable of identifying and removing false information in real-time. Additionally, social media companies must work towards creating a culture of responsibility and accountability among their users, by promoting critical thinking and media literacy skills, and encouraging users to fact-check information before sharing it.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": ["null"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Creating a robust AI-powered content moderation system is crucial for addressing the issue of fake content on social media. This system should be able to identify and flag false information in real-time, while also providing users with ways to report and remove such content. Additionally, platforms must invest in developing more sophisticated AI algorithms that can detect and prevent the spread of misinformation, rather than relying solely on human moderators who may not always be able to keep up with the sheer volume of content being shared online. Furthermore, social media companies must also work towards creating a culture of responsibility and accountability among their users, by promoting critical thinking and media literacy skills, and encouraging users to fact-check information before sharing it.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To create an effective AI-powered content moderation system, we must first identify the key factors that contribute to the spread of misinformation on social media. This includes understanding how AI algorithms can be used to detect and flag false information, as well as developing more sophisticated AI models that can predict the likelihood of a piece of content being fake. Once these factors are understood, we can begin to develop and implement a robust content moderation system that is capable of identifying and removing false information in real-time. Additionally, social media companies must work towards creating a culture of responsibility and accountability among their users, by promoting critical thinking and media literacy skills, and encouraging users to fact-check information before sharing it.
